etymology.description: 耳(耳) (ěr, 'ear') + 朵(朵) (duǒ, 'earlobe; measure word for flowers/clouds') = ear
etymology.interpretation: 耳(耳) (ěr, 'ear') refers to the organ used for hearing. 朵(朵) (duǒ, 'earlobe; measure word for flowers/clouds') contributes the idea of a distinct, soft, and possibly rounded part, enhancing the reference to the ear with an emphasis on its shape or form.

examples.chinese: 他的小狗有大耳朵(他的小狗有大耳朵)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiǎo gǒu yǒu dà ěrduo.

examples.chinese: 请不要大声喊，她的耳朵很敏感(請不要大聲喊，她的耳朵很敏感)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng búyào dàshēng hǎn, tā de ěrduo hěn mǐngǎn.
examples.english: Please don't shout loudly, her ears are very sensitive.

examples.chinese: 听音乐时，他喜欢戴上耳机以保护耳朵(聽音樂時，他喜歡戴上耳機以保護耳朵)。
examples.pinyin: Tīng yīnyuè shí, tā xǐhuān dàishang ěrjī yǐ bǎohù ěrduo.
examples.english: When listening to music, he likes to wear headphones to protect his ears.
